title: monardella odoratissima subsp. odoratissima
intro: Monardella odoratissima subsp. odoratissima, commonly known as pink clusters or pink monardella, is a flowering plant species in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb with fragrant leaves and pink, white, or purple flowers that are highly attractive to bees and butterflies. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and wounds. Infusions and tinctures are among the herbal preparations made from the plant, often used to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and support overall well-being.
